today.time: TBC — Microsoft 365 auth needed for exact time
today.synopsis: Weekly cross-functional product and design sync. Chloe posted in #core-product yesterday asking for agenda points — only her World Cup pipeline item has been added so far. Mike needs to add his points before the meeting.

discussion_topics.topic: World Cup pipeline — dev/UX support beyond what's been shared with Mike
discussion_topics.detail: Chloe's item: 'World cup requests other than what has been shared with Mike. Is there anything else in the pipeline that dev/ux support will be needed?' Mike briefed Chloe on 05/05: Group Bets (design target Thu 08/05), WC Predictor Game (Miguel to start this week, target Tue 12/05), Cosmo WC (design this week), Betting Tips Hub V1 (in dev, no concerns). Hard deadline: 11 June. KO call with devs agreed for Predictor + Group Bets.

discussion_topics.topic: GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign — Day 20 Blocked — unblock opportunity
discussion_topics.detail: Michael Butlin is in the meeting — ideal opportunity to address the blocker in person. Blocked since 16/04. Ciara Carroll also attends. Chloe confirmed on 29/04 it is 'beholden to Michael B'. Raise directly and get a clear answer or timeline today.

discussion_topics.topic: Sprint 170 velocity — 6 tickets Done in last 48h
discussion_topics.detail: Strong output yesterday: GDCU-8895 (Prediction Market US Sitemap — Done, was high priority), GDCU-8912 (Cosmo Spins rotate msg), GDCU-8854 (Geo selector bug), GDCU-8842 (Genesis Content Layouts), GDCU-8611 (Feature Tag), GDCU-7363 (Slots widget). In Product review queue: GDCU-8877 (new, Novica), GDCU-8736 (Ciara, Day 8). 8 tickets currently In QA.

discussion_topics.topic: Tinder Widget — removal and Group Bets replacement
discussion_topics.detail: Mike told Chloe on 05/05: 'It makes sense to remove the Tinder widget until further notice, and we will look to replace it with the Group Bets when that is ready.' Confirm GDCU-8546 is deprioritised in the sprint plan and the replacement timeline is clear.

today.prep_notes: URGENT — Add agenda points before the sync. Recommended items: (1) WC delivery plan: Group Bets design target Thu 08/05, Predictor design target Tue 12/05, hard deadline 11 June, KO call with devs to schedule; (2) GDCU-8619 blocker: Michael Butlin is in the room — get a direct answer; (3) Sprint 170 momentum: 6 Done in 48h, 8 In QA pipeline; (4) Tinder widget removal decision. Also brief the group: GDCU-8895 (US Sitemap) is now Done — no further action needed. Note: Vic Dadson not in the agenda attendees list; Miguel off yesterday. Gabriel Cornoiu is attending — good opportunity to close out the intake form questions (SEO & operator attributes from 23/04).

action_items.action: ADD PRODUCT SYNC AGENDA POINTS NOW — Chloe posted the agenda link in #core-product yesterday: https://gdcgroup.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/GDCC/pages/6609044188/Agenda+May+6th+2026. Currently only Chloe's WC item is there. Add Mike's points before the meeting: WC delivery plan (Group Bets by Thu, Predictor by Tue 12/05, June 11 deadline), GDCU-8619 blocker escalation (Michael Butlin is in the meeting — ideal unblock moment), sprint 170 highlights, Tinder removal/Group Bets replacement.

UNBLOCK IN MEETING — GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign (Ciara Carroll, Day 20 Blocked since 16/04). Michael Butlin is attending today's product sync — raise directly and get his decision in the room. This is the longest-standing blocker on the board. If Michael still cannot give direction, escalate to Colin Brannigan immediately after.

SCHEDULE WC KO CALL — Mike agreed with Chloe on 05/05 to run a kickoff call for World Cup Predictor Game and Group Bets with the dev team. Chloe confirmed 'the call with devs is crucial once the design is finished.' Group Bets design targets Thursday 08/05 — schedule the KO call for Friday 09/05 or early next week. Mike has sign-off from Dean and Michael already.

REPLY TO JESSICA — Banners decision still outstanding since 30/04 15:05 BST. Jessica: 'Let me know about the banners please so I'll check with Ciara after.' Relates to GDCU-8928 IUJ Banners. Note: Mike sent Jess a BBC Sport football quiz link on 05/05 15:18 (presumably WC Predictor research context), but the banners decision remains unaddressed. Resolve today.

REVIEW — GDCU-8877 Add Field to Manage Phone Numbers on Email Confirmation Pages in Admin Panel (Novica Vukobratovic, In Product, Medium). Moved to In Product yesterday 05/05 at 16:21 BST. New item in the review queue — 4 eye-flags suggest it has been watched. Review and sign off or provide feedback today.

CHECK VIC DADSON RETURN — Vic's fortnight sick leave ended yesterday (05/05). No Slack messages from her since 21/04. No check-in received yet this morning. Reach out gently — wellbeing first, then discuss the data engineering plan (Nemanja reqs + knowledge sharing from Chris in BI team, as agreed with Colin on 17/04). Review her open backlog items before reaching out.

CHECK MIGUEL IS BACK — Miguel was off yesterday (05/05, confirmed by him on 30/04). World Cup Predictor Game (GDCU-8935) design needs to start this week with a target of next Tuesday 12/05. Confirm Miguel is back and has the brief. If further briefing is needed, reference Mike's WC status update to Chloe from 05/05.

SIGN OFF — GDCU-8736 Learning Hub Imagery (Ciara Carroll, In Product, Medium, Day 8). Ciara shared Figma prototype. Chloe noted 'some back and forth' at sprint close 30/04. Approving unblocks Carlos Blauth on GDCU-8733 (Apply Feedback to Learning Hub). Ciara is in today's product sync — confirm any outstanding feedback before or during the meeting.

KARL CHECK-IN THIS WEEK — Mike told Chloe on 05/05: 'I have a check in with him this week to confirm' the data is exposed (re: World Cup matrix input, fixture dating & resulting — dependencies for both Group Bets and Predictor Game). Schedule or confirm this check-in today so the KO call with devs has the correct data picture.

CONFIRM TINDER WIDGET DEPRIORITISATION IN SPRINT — Mike told Chloe on 05/05 to remove the Tinder widget until further notice and replace with Group Bets when ready. GDCU-8546 still shows as 'In Progress' in the sprint. Update the ticket status and confirm with Chloe/the team that the sprint plan reflects this decision.

INTAKE FORM — Gabriel Cornoiu's suggestions from 23/04 (SEO & Information Architecture for new pages/hubs; operator offer attributes) still outstanding. Gabriel is attending today's product sync — good opportunity to confirm the intake form has been updated and align on these additions in person.

CREATIVE DIRECTION — GDCU-8846 Cosmo Spins Spring Ad Campaign (Ciara Carroll, To Do). Josh Kerr feedback: needs rethinking — clearer message, less 'gaming' feel. Ciara is also carrying GDCU-8619, GDCU-8736, and GDCU-8947 alongside WC work. Provide creative direction so this does not further age in Ciara's queue.

MONITOR — GDCU-8931 Strategy pages do not appear in search (Harden Thomas Wallace, In QA, Medium, 05/05 22:04). New ticket that moved to In QA overnight. No action needed from Mike at this stage but worth awareness given it affects the Strategy hub.
